heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 7th since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 9 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 23 December 2025.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (198°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 13 October 2012.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 7.8°C), outside temperature (down 4.7°C), and feels like temperature (down 3.8°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 294 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.5°C), outside temperature (up 8.7°C), cloud levels (up 337 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 293.9m lower than yesterday, 337.3m lower than the past week, 24.8m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 828m to 2280m and averaged 987.0m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; bitterly cold temperatures are expected. Also, the air will feel dry and little or no wind. Drizzle or light rain possible, occasional overcast conditions, and periods of sunshine are expected. Weather should be calm and steady.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-2.8°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
